root_cause: Check-then-act (TOCTOU) race in the idempotency layer: IdempotencyStore.lookup() and IdempotencyStore.reserve() are two non-atomic operations with the PSP charge in between, and nothing provides per-key mutual exclusion. Two concurrent requests with the same Idempotency-Key both observe a cache MISS and both proceed to charge.
source_location: app/payments/idempotency.py - the lookup() / reserve() split (gate at line 48); idempotency table has an index but NO UNIQUE constraint on idempotency_key.
symptom: Two PSP charges for one idempotency key (duplicate customer debit) under concurrency.
confirmed_by: Backward trace from the second psp_charge to the missing critical section + a one-variable probe: a per-key lock across lookup+reserve drops the duplicate rate from 74% to 0% with nothing else changed.
why_intermittent: The vulnerable window [lookup .. reserve] is ~2ms wide; duplicates require two same-key requests to overlap inside it, which only happens under load (mobile client retries an in-flight request on a 2s timeout).
not_the_fix: PSP-side dedupe / retry handling (treats the symptom; the local race remains and other side effects stay unguarded)
client-side debounce (cannot guarantee server-side once-only; other clients exist)
a global/process lock (kills throughput AND is per-process - multiple workers each hold their own, so it does not actually serialize across pods)
raising cache TTL or moving to Redis (does not make check-and-claim atomic)
correct_fix_shape: Make check-and-claim a single atomic operation at the shared store (reserve-or-get), backed by a UNIQUE constraint. Detailed in fix_design.
source: systematic-debugging Iron Law ('NO FIXES WITHOUT ROOT CAUSE INVESTIGATION FIRST') + root-cause-tracing.md ('fix at source, not symptom')
